title: System Design for Beginners (Full Guide)
summary: AWS tutorial introduces a framework for system design interviews using seven steps: requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level component design, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The approach emphasizes problem-first thinking rather than technology-first design.
writeup: This AWS Developers tutorial lays out a framework for system design interviews built on a sequence of steps: requirements gathering, traffic estimation, high-level component design, API contracts, tech stack selection, and implementation constraints. The central argument is problem-first thinking, defining requirements and constraints before choosing technology rather than starting with a stack. Takeaways include estimating traffic to size components, defining API contracts between parts of the system, and selecting a tech stack only after the problem is scoped.
